response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer (13+ years) with strong web/backend credentials, but the role demands a dedicated C++ developer with 8-12 years of C++ depth, networking/multi-threading, message brokers, MQTT, and IEC 62443 industrial cybersecurity exposure. His C++ experience is limited to a 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015; the rest of his career is JavaScript/TypeScript/DLang/Node.js. No evidence of industrial automation, SDL/IEC 62443, MQTT, or relevant protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, etc.). Skills list does not even include C++.
strengths: Long engineering tenure with cross-stack breadth (web, mobile, desktop, embedded)
Strong TDD, CI/CD, and software-craftsmanship discipline aligned with quality mindset
Some prior C/C++ exposure at National Instruments on engineering tooling (LabWindows/CVI)
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~6 months, a decade ago) and absent from current skill set
No demonstrated industrial automation, MQTT, message-broker, or IEC 62443/SDL background
Career trajectory is firmly in TypeScript/Node/DLang web ecosystems, not high-performance C++ systems

key_factors.factor: Insufficient C++ depth for senior C++ role
No industrial automation / IEC 62443 / MQTT experience
Strong general engineering seniority and craftsmanship
